meaning: adv.不适当地,过度地,不正当地
examples: This will achieve greater security without unduly burdening the consumers or the economy.
Free-marketeers would argue that governments do not need to intervene in the currency and interest rate process unduly.
Detaining and ticketing such drivers is not the purpose of the roadblock and would unduly slow down the stops of other cars.
Enterprises unduly focus on the low-level technologies and the process of retrieving the data.
Until now this policy has been unduly loose for Spain and Ireland, exacerbating their housing booms.
